articles.title: U.S. And Iran Enter Technical Talks To Secure Peace Deal, Restart Shipping
articles.content:
多哈/迪拜/弗吉尼亚州弗吉尼亚比奇，7月1日（路透社）——伊朗和美国周三结束了一轮间接谈判，没有迹象表明双方在实现持久和平方面取得进展，而是专注于两国称两周前宣布的临时协议已经解决的问题。

知情人士称，两国谈判代表在多哈进行了为期两天的会谈，讨论霍尔木兹海峡的海上交通和解除伊朗资金冻结问题，这是初始协议下的两个关键议题。

卡塔尔外交部表示，下一次会议将在伊朗已故最高领袖哈梅内伊大阿亚图拉的葬礼仪式之后举行，他将于7月9日下葬。

卡塔尔外交部发言人在X平台上发帖称，多哈会谈在6月停战协议相关问题上取得了“积极进展”，并正在“延续”瑞士峰会的成果。

在美国方面，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普表示，双方在伊朗核计划限制问题上取得了进展——这是他与以色列在2月发动战争的主要原因。他对媒体表示：“伊朗去核化进展顺利。他们进行了非常好的会谈，我们会看到的。”

然而，知情人士表示，核计划并未在技术性会谈中提及。

美国副总统JD·万斯表示，该问题将在稍后处理。他对媒体称：“很明显，我们对核问题感到担忧，我们将开始讨论这个问题。”

卡塔尔外交部表示，美国和伊朗谈判代表分别与卡塔尔和巴基斯坦调解人举行了会议。

一位匿名消息人士透露，特朗普的女婿贾里德·库什纳和美国最高特使史蒂夫·威特科夫被派往该地区参加白宫所称的“高级别”会谈，但两人并未出席这些会议。

伊朗代表团团长、副外交部长卡泽姆·加里巴巴迪表示，会谈已经结束。双方都没有透露他们是否成功弥合了任何分歧。

谁掌控霍尔木兹海峡？

初步协议要求伊朗和美国允许霍尔木兹海峡的航运恢复，该海峡在战争前承载了全球五分之一的石油和液化天然气贸易。尽管交通已部分恢复，但这条战略水道的状况仍不明朗，上周末在伊朗袭击一艘货船后，两国交换了打击。

两名伊朗高级消息人士称，伊朗决心赢得国际社会对其控制该海峡的承认，即使必须通过武力实现，并多次表示将在8月中旬开始对航运征收通行费，此前为初始协议规定的免通行费期。

特朗普周三的言论淡化了与伊朗重新爆发全面战争的可能性。他称：“我认为他们已经取得了很大进展。”

受特朗普言论影响，石油价格跌至四个月来的最低水平，分析师自战争开始以来首次下调了价格预测。

伊朗国家媒体周三报道，一艘外国集装箱船在伊朗当局指定的航道外的浅水区搁浅。

石油市场分析提供商Vanda Insights的创始人范达娜·哈里表示：“霍尔木兹海峡继续重新开放，但情况零散、不可预测，而且不完全透明。”

几个欧洲国家已提出帮助清除海峡中的水雷，但德国国防部长鲍里斯·皮斯托瑞斯表示，他预计德国不会参与，理由是伊朗不愿与其他国家合作。
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

DOHA/DUBAI/VIRGINIA BEACH, Virginia, July 1 (Reuters) - Iran and the United States concluded a round of indirect talks on Wednesday with no sign they had made headway toward a lasting peace, focusing instead on issues that they said had been resolved when an interim agreement was announced two weeks ago.

Sources familiar with the discussions said negotiators for the two countries spent two days in Doha discussing maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz and unfreezing Iran’s funds, two critical issues under the initial agreement.

Advertisement

The next meeting will take place after funeral processions for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who is due to be buried on July 9, Qatar’s Foreign Ministry said.

The Doha discussions produced “positive progress” on issues related to the memorandum that halted the war in June and were “building on the outcomes” of a summit in Switzerland, the ministry spokesperson said in a post on X.

In Washington, U.S. President Donald Trump said the two sides were making progress on possible limits to Iran’s nuclear program — the main reason he launched the war along with Israel in February. “The denuclearization of Iran is moving along well,” he told reporters. “They’ve had very good meetings, and we’ll see.”

Advertisement

But the sources said the nuclear program did not come up in the talks, which were technical in nature.

U.S. Vice President JD Vance said that matter would be addressed later. “Obviously, we’re worried about the nuclear issue, we’re going to start talking about that,” he told reporters.

American and Iranian negotiators held separate meetings with Qatari and Pakistani mediators, Qatar’s foreign ministry said.

Advertisement

Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner and top U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff, dispatched to the region for what the White House had billed as “high-level” talks, did not attend the sessions, according to a source who spoke on condition of anonymity.

The leader of Iran’s delegation, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi, said the talks concluded. Neither side said whether they had managed to bridge any of their differences.

Advertisement

Who Controls The Strait?

The initial deal calls for Iran and the United States to allow shipping to resume through the Strait of Hormuz, which handled one-fifth of global oil and liquid natural gas trade before the war. Though traffic has partially resumed, the status of the strategic waterway remains unclear and the two countries exchanged strikes last weekend following an Iranian attack on a cargo ship.

Iran is determined to win international recognition of its control over the strait even if it has to do so by force, two senior Iranian sources said, and has repeatedly said it will assess tolls on shipping starting in mid-August, after a toll-free period specified by the initial agreement expires.

Trump’s comments on Wednesday played down the possibility of a return to all-out war with Iran. “I think they’ve come a long way,” he said.

Advertisement

Oil prices fell to their lowest level in four months following Trump’s remarks, and analysts cut their price forecasts for the first time since the war began.

Iran’s state media said on Wednesday that a foreign container ship had run aground in shallow waters outside the shipping route designated by Iranian authorities.

“Hormuz continues to reopen but it’s patchy, unpredictable, and not fully transparent,” said Vandana Hari, founder of oil market analysis provider Vanda Insights.

Advertisement

Several European countries have offered to help clear mines from the Strait, but Germany’s defense minister Boris Pistorius said he did not expect his country to participate, citing Iran’s unwillingness to cooperate with other countries.

Related

president donald trump

j.d. vance

Iran war

jared kushner

steve witkoff

Iran Says It Won't Meet With U.S. Envoys, Clouding Prospects For Peace Deal
Iran World Cup Team Leaves Farewell Message About 'Humanity And Dignity'
U.S., Iran Reach Deal To End The War, Reopen Strait Of Hormuz
Trump Didn’t Get Much Out Of The Iran War Peace Deal

articles.title: Guterres Warns U.N. Agency For Palestinian Refugees Nearing 'Breaking Point'
articles.content:
6月30日（路透社）——联合国秘书长古特雷斯当日敦促各国填补联合国近东巴勒斯坦难民救济和工程处（UNRWA）1亿美元的经费缺口，称该机构在大幅削减成本和紧缩措施后已接近崩溃临界点。

古特雷斯在联大自愿捐款特别会议上表示，由于被占领巴勒斯坦领土各地实施全面限制措施阻碍其工作，加上严重的资金短缺，UNRWA的处境日益不稳。

这家联合国机构在加沙、约旦河西岸、黎巴嫩、约旦和叙利亚运营，向260万巴勒斯坦人提供援助、教育、医疗保健、社会服务和庇护。

美国曾是UNRWA最大捐助方，但在2024年1月削减了资金，此前以色列指控约12名UNRWA员工参与了2023年10月7日哈马斯武装分子的致命袭击，这场袭击引发了加沙战争。瑞典也削减了2025年资金。其他主要捐助方在哈马斯相关指控调查期间暂停了资助，但大多数已恢复捐款。

古特雷斯说，该机构的流动性危机危及其履行使命的能力，而联大六个月前以压倒性支持重新授权其任务。

古特雷斯说：“没有成员国的紧急支持和资金援助，他们无法继续这样运转。”他指出，该机构已采取果断措施实施改革，并更新了关于外部和政治活动的政策，以回应以色列的指控。

“UNRWA是动荡时代的稳定力量，”他说，并驳斥了他所称的持续试图通过“虚假信息、抹黑运动、立法行动、运营限制、外交障碍等手段”削弱该机构的努力。

古特雷斯说，这些行动威胁到数百万巴勒斯坦人和UNRWA员工的福祉。他指出，自2023年10月以来，加沙已有390名UNRWA员工遇难。他注意到，自10月宣布停火以来，已有1000名巴勒斯坦人在以色列袭击中丧生。

服务缩减

联合国表示，已解雇了9名可能参与2023年10月袭击的UNRWA员工，那次袭击造成约1200名以色列人和外国公民死亡。9月在黎巴嫩被以色列击毙的一名哈马斯指挥官也曾拥有UNRWA职位。联合国否认与哈马斯存在联系，并誓言调查所有指控。

古特雷斯说，UNRWA今年已将其服务交付时间减少了20%，削减了当地人员工资，并让15%的国际职位空缺。他补充道：“任何进一步削减都可能将条件推向崩溃临界点。”

联合国发言人斯特凡·杜加里克表示，该机构面临生存危机。他说，关于自愿捐款特别会议的结果将于周三公布。

根据其网站数据，2025年UNRWA获得约8.87亿美元承诺捐款和8.29亿美元实际捐款，仅占33亿美元总需求资金的27%。

（报道：安德里亚·沙哈尔；编辑：保罗·西马奥）
articles.summary:

June 30 (Reuters) - U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Tuesday urged countries to cover a $100 million gap in funding for the U.N. Palestinian refugee agency UNRWA, saying the body was nearing a breaking point after deep cost-cutting and austerity measures.

Guterres told an ad hoc meeting of the General Assembly on voluntary contributions that UNRWA’s situation was increasingly precarious given sweeping restrictions throughout occupied Palestinian territory that impeded its work, and the large cash shortfall.

Advertisement

The United Nations agency operates in Gaza, the West Bank, Lebanon, Jordan and Syria, providing aid, schooling, healthcare, social services and shelter to 2.6 million Palestinians.

The U.S. was UNRWA’s biggest donor, but cut funding in January 2024 after Israel accused about a dozen UNRWA staff of taking part in the deadly October 7, 2023, attack by Hamas militants that triggered the war in Gaza. Sweden also cut its 2025 funding. Other big donors paused funding for UNRWA while the Hamas charges were investigated, but most have resumed their contributions.

Advertisement

Guterres said the agency’s liquidity crisis jeopardized its ability to meet its mandate, which was renewed by the General Assembly six months ago with overwhelming member support.

“They cannot keep going like this without urgent backing and financial support from member states,” Guterres said, noting that the agency had taken decisive steps to implement reforms and update its policy on outside and political activities following Israel’s accusations.

“UNRWA is a stabilizing force in an age of instability,” he said, rejecting what he called continued efforts to undermine the agency through “disinformation, smear campaigns, legislative actions, operational restrictions, diplomatic roadblocks and more.”

Advertisement

Such actions threatened the well-being of millions of Palestinians as well as UNRWA staff, Guterres said, noting that 390 UNRWA staff had been killed in Gaza since October 2023. He noted that 1,000 Palestinians had been killed in Israeli attacks since a ceasefire announced in October.

Service Delivery Reductions

The U.N. has said it fired nine UNRWA staff who may have been involved in the October 2023 attack, which killed about 1,200 Israelis and foreign nationals. A Hamas commander in Lebanon, killed in September by Israel, was also found to have had a UNRWA job. The U.N. has disputed links with Hamas and vowed to investigate all accusations.

UNRWA had reduced its service delivery hours by 20% this year, cutting salaries for local personnel and keeping 15% of international posts vacant, Guterres said, adding, “Any further cuts could push conditions past the breaking point.”

Advertisement

U.N. spokesman Stephane Dujarric said the agency faced an existential crisis. He said the results of the ad hoc meeting on voluntary contributions would be announced on Wednesday.

In 2025, UNRWA received about $887 million in pledges and $829 million in contributions, according to its website, accounting for just 27% of total funding needs of $3.3 billion.

(Reporting by Andrea Shalal; Editing by Paul Simao)

Advertisement

Related

United Nations

Israel Targeted Gaza Children Resulting In Genocide, UN Inquiry Says
Palestinian Death Toll In Gaza Tops 73,000 As Israel Continues Launching Strikes Despite Ceasefire
Israeli Strikes Kill At Least 10 In Gaza, Including Children
JD Vance Unloads On Israel For Not Backing 'Only Powerful Ally' It Has Left

articles.title: Russia Approved Secret China Military Training At Top Level: Reuters
articles.content:
7月1日（路透社）——据两名欧洲官员和路透社看到的文件显示，中国去年秘密为俄罗斯军队提供军事训练，这些训练由俄罗斯总统普京的国防部长亲自批准，直接涉及至少四名俄罗斯和中国将军。

这些官员表示，如此高级别人员参与与乌克兰战争相关的训练，表明俄中两国对这种合作高度重视。此事已引起欧洲警觉，尽管北京方面否认了这一事件。

路透社看到的一份俄罗斯机密文件直接提到国防部长安德烈·别洛乌索夫于2025年8月发布的一项内部法令。

文件称，根据别洛乌索夫的决定，俄罗斯武装部队代表团前往中国，在中国人民解放军设施参加训练演习。

辐射、生物、化学战训练

同一份报告详细描述了其中一项训练课程——去年11月在北京一处军事设施进行的为期三周的辐射、化学和生物防护培训。

该报告及第二份报告描述并展示了俄罗斯士兵接受中国教员授课、查看核反应堆模型、学习“化学侦察”、“辐射侦察”以及防护通风系统免受污染的场景。

其中一位欧洲官员表示，辐射、生物和化学战训练的纳入凸显了这些交流的战略性质，并指出这一主题对各国军队而言尤为敏感。

俄罗斯和中国的国防部均未回应置评请求。

中国外交部在一份声明中表示，中国在乌克兰危机问题上的立场始终如一。

“相关指控毫无根据，”声明补充道，并提及本报告中的细节。

北京方面称自己在俄罗斯与乌克兰的战争中保持中立，并将自己定位为和平调解者。

据路透社上月报道，援引欧洲情报机构和军事文件，中国去年11月培训了约200名俄罗斯军事人员，其中一些人此后已赴乌克兰参战。

克里姆林宫拒绝对该报道置评，但抱怨西方发布了“虚假信息”。

欧盟外交政策负责人卡贾·卡拉斯6月15日表示，布鲁塞尔已通过自身渠道确认培训确实发生，目前正在评估其影响。

北京方面称她的言论“纯属抹黑”。

欧盟权衡对贸易伙伴中国的回应

自2022年俄罗斯入侵乌克兰以来一直将俄罗斯视为主要安全威胁的欧洲大国，对世界第二大经济体和欧盟重要贸易伙伴中国与莫斯科关系日益密切保持着警惕。

对于这个27国集团而言，闭门讨论的核心在于：考虑到传统上决定中欧关系的贸易优先事项，是否需要针对此次培训采取进一步措施。

欧盟已对据称支持俄罗斯战争努力的中国企业实施制裁。

布鲁塞尔第三名官员告诉路透社，欧盟必须停止主要从经济角度看待中国，而应关注卡拉斯所称的其在俄罗斯战争中的“决定性推手”角色。

两名欧洲官员（因信息敏感性要求匿名）确认，支撑此次培训的7月2日协议签署人为俄罗斯少将鲁斯塔姆·胡赛诺夫和中国上校孙大运。

俄罗斯议会国防委员会主席、高级议员安德烈·卡尔塔波洛夫告诉俄罗斯RTVI电视台，关于训练的报道“完全是胡说八道”，俄罗斯军队没有什么需要向中国学习的。

中国缺乏实战经验

俄罗斯在乌克兰超过四年的战斗中积累了丰富经验，而中国虽然拥有规模庞大、技术先进的军队，但已有数十年未参与战争。

路透社看到的俄罗斯内部军事报告记录了训练的优缺点。

一份关于南京训练的报告称赞了设备标准、模拟器使用以及教员的高理论水平，同时特别指出中国缺乏实战经验。

其他文件列出了参与的三名将军。

路透社看到的俄罗斯军事文件列出了所有课程参与者的姓名，包括高级军官的姓名，并提供了军衔、出生日期、隶属单位和安全级别。

根据名单和路透社看到的第二份军事文件，俄罗斯地面部队副总司令大将鲁斯塔姆·穆拉多夫率领俄罗斯代表团。

根据后者，中国少将、解放军辐射化学生物防护军事学院院长李金孙参加了其中一门课程的开幕式。

据名单显示，俄罗斯少将维塔利·格拉西莫夫在蚌埠参加了一门课程。

（编辑：迈克·科利特-怀特和凯文·利菲）
articles.summary:
LOADINGERROR LOADING
&

July 1 (Reuters) - China’s covert military training of Russian forces last year was personally approved by President Vladimir Putin’s defense minister and directly involved at least four Russian and Chinese generals, according to two European officials and documents seen by Reuters.

The officials said the involvement of such high-ranking individuals in training linked to the Ukraine war signaled the importance for Russia and China of such cooperation, which has caused alarm in Europe even as Beijing has denied it took place.

Advertisement

A classified Russian document seen by Reuters directly referred to an internal decree issued by Defense Minister Andrei Belousov in August, 2025.

It said that, in accordance with a decision by Belousov, a delegation from Russia’s armed forces travelled to China to participate in training exercises at People’s Liberation Army (PLA) facilities.

Training in Radiological, Biological, Chemical Warfare

The same report detailed one of the training courses - a three-week session focused on radiological, chemical and biological protection at a military facility in Beijing in November.

Advertisement

The report and a second one described and displayed images of Russian soldiers being lectured by a Chinese instructor, looking at a model nuclear reactor, and being taught about “chemical reconnaissance”, “radiation reconnaissance” and protecting ventilation systems from contamination.

The inclusion of radiological, biological and chemical warfare training underlined the strategic nature of the exchanges, one of the European officials said, noting that the topic was particularly sensitive for militaries in general.

The defense ministries of Russia and China did not respond to requests for comment for this article.

China’s foreign ministry said in a statement that its stance on the Ukraine crisis had remained consistent.

Advertisement

“The relevant allegations are entirely unfounded,” it added, referring to details contained in this report.

Beijing says it is neutral in Russia’s war with Ukraine, and presents itself as a peace mediator.

According to a Reuters report last month citing European intelligence agencies and military documents, China in November trained around 200 Russian military personnel, some of whom have since joined the war in Ukraine.

Advertisement

The Kremlin declined to comment on that report, but complained about “false information” published in the West.

European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said on June 15 that Brussels had confirmed through its own channels that the training had taken place and was now assessing the implications.

Beijing described her comments as “nothing but smears”.

EU Ponders Response To Trade Partner China

European powers, which have viewed Russia as their main security threat since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, have watched warily as ties have grown closer between Moscow and China, the world’s second largest economy and a key EU trade partner.

Advertisement

For the 27-member bloc, discussion behind closed doors centers around whether further measures are needed in response to the training, given the trade priorities that traditionally shape the relationship with Beijing.

The EU has already imposed sanctions on Chinese companies that it says support Russia’s war effort.

A third official, in Brussels, told Reuters the bloc had to stop viewing China primarily through an economic lens, but focus on what Kallas called its role as a “decisive enabler of Russia’s war”.

Both of the European officials, who asked not to be named because of the sensitivity of the information, identified the signatories of a July 2 agreement underpinning the training as Russian Major General Rustam Khusainov and Chinese Senior Colonel Sun Dayun.

Advertisement

Andrei Kartapolov, a senior lawmaker who heads the Russian parliament’s defense committee, told Russia’s RTVI outlet that the report about the training was “complete nonsense” and that Russia’s military had nothing to learn from China.

China’s Lack Of Combat Experience

Russia has accrued extensive experience in more than four years of combat in Ukraine, while China, with a vast and technologically advanced military, has not fought a war in decades.

Internal Russian military reports seen by Reuters noted strengths and weaknesses in the training.

One report on the training in Nanjing praised the standard of the equipment, the use of simulators and the instructors’ high theoretical knowledge while specifically noting China’s lack of combat experience.

Advertisement

Other documents named three generals who took part.

One Russian military document seen by Reuters listed the names of every participant in all of the courses - including those of senior officers - providing rank, date of birth, affiliation and level of security clearance in each case.

Colonel General Rustam Muradov, deputy commander-in-chief of Russia’s land forces, led the Russian delegation, according to the list and a second military document seen by Reuters.

According to the latter, Chinese Major General Li Jinsun, head of the PLA’s Military Academy of Radiological, Chemical and Biological Defence, took part in the opening of one of the courses.

Advertisement

Russian Major General Vitaly Gerasimov took part in a course in Bengbu, according to the list.

(Editing by Mike Collett-White and Kevin Liffey)

Related

China
